moveAnnotations.annotation: Welcome to the Polish Opening, an unconventional but strategically rich way to start the game! We're immediately staking a claim on the c5 and d4 squares by advancing our queenside pawn, preparing to fianchetto our light-squared bishop on b2—a setup that controls the long diagonal and targets d4 without occupying it with a pawn. This opening prioritizes piece activity and flexibility over classical central control, letting Black commit first while we maintain multiple plans: we can push c4 to support the center, develop our bishop actively, and keep our central pawns flexible. The Polish Opening appeals to creative players who understand positional ideas well, as it requires patience and accurate follow-up rather than relying on forcing moves.
